challenges.expected_evidence: Output PDF contains a FreeText annotation with the correct text at the transformed coordinates.
challenges.failure_signal: Annotation is missing or placed at an incorrect location.
rubric: Adds FreeText annotation to the correct location.
challenges.task: Fill a PDF with a simple text annotation at specific coordinates derived from fields.json.

challenges.expected_evidence: Text annotations align precisely with the intended positions on varied PDF sizes.
challenges.failure_signal: Misalignment of text fields across different page dimensions.
rubric: Correctly scales bounding boxes from image coordinates to PDF coordinates.
Handles different page widths and heights.
challenges.task: Handle coordinate transformation for different PDF page sizes and resolutions.

challenges.expected_evidence: Output PDF contains an annotation for every field defined in fields.json.
rubric: Reads and processes all field definitions.
Maintains correct ordering and location for each field.
challenges.task: Process multiple fields from fields.json and add all annotations to the PDF.

constraints: The PDF must have existing page dimensions known for transformation.
The JSON must have bounding boxes in image coordinates.
Only FreeText annotations are supported (not text fields or checkboxes).
facts: The script uses pypdf's PdfReader and PdfWriter.
Field definitions are stored in fields.json.
The transform_coordinates function converts bounding box from image coordinates to PDF coordinates.
The script likely runs from command line with JSON input.
procedures: Read fields.json and extract field data.
For each field, transform coordinates using transform_coordinates.
Add FreeText annotation to the PDF page.
context_pack.summary: This script fills a PDF form by reading field definitions from a JSON file and adding FreeText annotations using pypdf. It includes a coordinate transformation function to convert image coordinates to PDF coordinates.
